title:
Lesson 1:
Following & Waiting
description: 'Bana eşlik eder misin?' = Will you come with me? 'Beni takip et' = Follow me. 'Beni bekle' = Wait for me. These are common phrases for coordinating movement with someone.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Markete gidiyorum. Bana eşlik eder misin?
dialogue.english: I'm going to the market. Will you come with me?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Beni takip et. Bir kısa yol biliyorum.
dialogue.english: Follow me. I know a shortcut.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Wait for me, I need to grab my keys.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Okay, I'll wait for you here.

title:
Lesson 2:
Hurry Up / Take It Easy
description: 'Acele et' = Hurry up. 'Sakin ol' = Take it easy / Calm down. These opposites are very common in everyday Turkish speech.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Hurry up! The bus is coming.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Take it easy, there's still time.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: No, it leaves in five minutes.

title:
Lesson 3:
A Moment Please
description: 'Bir dakika izin ver' = Give me a moment / Excuse me a moment. 'Bir dakikanızı alabilir miyim?' = May I have a moment of your time? (formal). 'Bir dakika ver bana' = Give me a moment. 'Bir şey yap' = Do something.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Bir dakika izin ver, bir telefon var.
dialogue.english: Excuse me a moment, I have a call.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.turkish: Bir dakika daha ver bana lütfen.
dialogue.english: Give me one more moment please.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Tamam. Bu arada bir şey yap, boş durma.
dialogue.english: Okay. In the meantime, do something, don't just stand there.
